PRIOR ART: Lit Playground (google/playground-elements) is THE closest analog — also web-components-first, also live-executes — and chose iframe `srcdoc` isolation (own execution context; console captured by monkey-patching console.* + postMessage to parent) + rewriting bare imports to CDN URLs (`import 'lit'`→unpkg/esm.sh). CodeSandbox Sandpack = iframe bundler (heavier); StackBlitz WebContainers = full Node-in-browser (maximalist); esm.sh/`esm.sh/tsx` = the no-build runtime-source + edge TSX-compile mechanism Lit-style tools point at; esbuild-wasm = `esbuild.transform()` in a Web Worker (initialize()+WASM url) and the only single-call transform that ALSO does module resolution/bundling (~10x slower than native, fine here); @babel/standalone lacks module resolution (weaker for a wrapper that imports react). LOAD-BEARING PLATFORM FACTS grounding the defaults: import maps are document-scoped and resolve-once (a fresh `<iframe srcdoc>` gets a fresh import map per preview; same-doc cannot re-resolve frameworks across previews/targets); customElements.define throws on re-definition (same-doc can only reuse the workbench's already-registered element, an iframe gets a clean registry); executed wrapper code is framework code not host DOM (a same-doc throw can break the workbench shell; an iframe contains it). HOW IT RESHAPES THE FORKS: Fork A (element isolation) — Lit Playground + bias-toward-separation move the best-end-state default from the item's interim A2 (same-document) to A1 (iframe srcdoc + own import-map); the genuine counterweight keeping A2 live is that A1 must get the element DEFINED inside the frame, needing a stable per-block module URL the registry doesn't expose (real build cost in #912) — but that is build effort (prioritization, filed to the build per fork-is-not-a-prioritization-tool), not a merit defect, so the fork rules A1 on merit. Fork B (framework-runtime source) — invariant: the runtime must NEVER enter FUI's shipped bundle (B-bundle-into-FUI is the flawed excluded branch, a web-components lib shipping framework runtimes). Among framework-free options the item defaulted to CDN import-map (esm.sh), but the item ALSO requires an e2e plan not dependent on external network ('vendor the runtimes or stub'); if vendoring must exist for CI anyway, CDN means maintaining TWO paths (CDN dev + vendored CI), so the merit default moves to a single LOCALLY-VENDORED devDependency served into the sandbox iframe — offline + deterministic, framework-free (a devDep is not the shipped bundle), and (paired with the already-local esbuild-wasm) one path for dev + CI. The two forks are COUPLED: B's framework-free isolation is cleanest when the runtime loads INSIDE the iframe (A1), not the workbench document (A2); A1 + local-vendored mirrors Lit Playground while honoring framework-free + offline-CI. CLASSIFICATION: FUI-owned devtool inside the workbench (fui:workbench/, locus frontierui) — not a WE standard (impl-is-not-a-standard, #809 workbench is FUI-owned); no new protocol (consumes only the ratified CEM #626 the wrapper is generated from, + #506 verdict/#891 runner for the downstream badge #913); no runtime-DI seam (author/dev-time execution, not a registry the running standard consults); transpiler stays swappable impl. RECOMMENDATION: Fork A→A1 iframe srcdoc (~70%; residual: needs a per-block module URL + the #815 one-document-no-channel philosophy is A2's principled counter — flag for skeptic pass); Fork B→local-vendored devDep served to the sandbox iframe (~75%; residual: esm.sh CDN is less wiring and is what Lit Playground ships); supported-by-default: esbuild-wasm in a Web Worker, console/error via the Lit-Playground postMessage monkey-patch, e2e reuses the vendored runtime (no external network — resolves the item's e2e ask). Full grounding in reports/2026-06-18-polyglot-live-test-sandbox.md.

title: Polyglot panel — what verdict backs a per-target *conformance* badge (source-golden vs behavioral vs health)
summary: Prep research for decision #913 (Block Explorer polyglot-panel slice c, epic #746; sibling of behavioral badge #967 = #954 Fork 2): what deterministic verdict, if any, can back a pass/fail CONFORMANCE badge on each genWrapper target tab (react/vue) — or should the badge just be the behavioral one (#967)? The card was filed assuming an 'already-committed deterministic #506 verdict' backs it; tracing to the tree shows that verdict does NOT exist for react/vue. GROUNDING (verified): panel targets are react/vue only (fui:workbench/mount.ts:648 TARGET_LABELS; TARGETS from fui:tools/gen-wrapper/genWrapper.mjs:26; slice-c reservation comment mount.ts:635); genWrapper is a pure (decl,target)=>string (genWrapper.mjs:207-212) emitting SOURCE ONLY — no diagnostics, no lossy flag, no conformance metadata, and no committed source golden exists (tests are substring assertions, not snapshots); the committed #506 golden (we:blocks/renderers/module-service/conformance/golden.json) is 14 MaaS/HTTP X-MaaS vectors for reference-JS vs .NET — ZERO react/vue content, a different axis; the ONLY react/vue conformance artifact is BEHAVIORAL — we:wrapper-conformance/runner.ts+vectors.ts, five contract vectors executed over a LIVE mounted WrapperSubject (runner.ts:37-46 requires render()->host element, dispatches real DOM events) = exactly #967's scope (blockedBy [912,954], needs the #912 live sandbox). PRIOR ART: (1) custom-elements-everywhere.com — THE canonical per-framework conformance badge; methodology is BEHAVIORAL (Karma in real browsers checking display/bind/children/events); React's '100% as of v19' badge is a runtime behavioral result, not a source snapshot — the industry's 'wrapper/interop conformance badge' is behavioral by definition. (2) Stencil output targets (closest analog: CEM->react/vue wrappers) — ecosystem wisdom is explicit that snapshot-of-generated-source and behavioral assertions cover DIFFERENT risks and are complementary: a source golden catches generator DRIFT, only behavioral verifies the wrapper actually forwards props/events. (3) Snapshot/golden testing generally is a REGRESSION signal ('output unchanged unless intentionally changed'), NOT a behavior/conformance guarantee. SYNTHESIS to #913 (separating truthful-claim from cost): A (deterministic wrapper-source golden) = a generator-REGRESSION signal, honest only if labelled 'source-stable'; behind a 'conformance' label it over-claims, same honesty flaw as C — a legit independent artifact but not the conformance badge. B (collapse #913's deterministic-conformance badge into #967's behavioral one) = the only genuine per-target conformance signal, confirmed by tree (sole react/vue artifact is behavioral) AND prior art 1; #954 Fork 2 split deterministic-vs-behavioral on a premise (a deterministic verdict exists) that is false, so they re-merge — RECOMMENDED DEFAULT ~75-80%. C (cheap genWrapper health: emit-success/no-lossy/no-diagnostics) = genWrapper emits no such metadata today (bare string) so nothing to read without building it first, and 'generated cleanly' is not conformant — weakest. CLASSIFICATION: badge=FUI workbench UI (locus:frontierui set); vectors=WE-owned contract (we:wrapper-conformance/vectors.ts); the live-subject runner=impl->FUI (impl-is-not-a-standard, #817 'a running handler is impl'); under B only vectors cross WE->FUI, honoring #700 — clean placement matching #967's locus+blockedBy. RESIDUAL for decider: whether a sandbox-independent deterministic tier is wanted enough to keep a RELABELLED 'source-stable' badge (A, honestly framed) as a SECOND badge alongside behavioral (Stencil 'both cover different risks' lesson), vs. #913 redundant with #967 and a source-golden filed separately as a generator-regression test. Full grounding in reports/2026-06-18-polyglot-conformance-badge-backing.md.

title: MaaS wrapper-serve protocol — does the CEM->framework-wrapper ESM serve contract need defining, or does servePathIR already cover it?
summary: Prep research for decision #974 (blocks the live-test sandbox #912; Block Explorer epic #746, locus frontierui). #974 was filed to DEFINE the MaaS wrapper-serve protocol — the CEM->framework-wrapper ESM serve contract a browser sandbox consumes via plain import() — on the premise that 'that protocol is not yet defined.' THE PREMISE IS FALSE (the #792/webexpressions 'assumed-greenfield, already-built' trap): the MaaS serve-path IR (blocks/renderers/module-service/servePathIR.ts, ratified #505/#463) is a language-neutral zero-import source of truth that ALREADY defines the URL grammar (<name>[@<pin>].js), the form/target/strategy params, content-hash identity, immutable/floating cache, ETag/SRI headers, and the 400/404/500 error set — and a React 'functional component + element wrapper' form ALREADY ships (moduleService.ts ServeForm = declarative|wc-class|html|jsx|functional, the functional blurb is literally 'A React-style functional component + element wrapper; transpiled to ESM'). EVERY #974 sub-question maps to an existing artifact: request (block id+framework[+CEM hash]) = SERVE_PATH.route + params (name=block id, pin=CEM hash, framework=the one fork); response (ESM+content-type+cache/version headers) = MEDIA_TYPES/CACHE_POLICY/MAAS_HEADERS (X-MaaS-Producer/-Integrity/-Lossy); bare react/vue import resolution = #081 native-import contract (spec-clean ESM + import-map, no consumer build) + #955 Fork B (FUI bundler/devDep resolves runtime); error/404 = SERVE_PATH.responses (400 unknown form, 404 no component/non-MaaS path/stale pin, 500 transform threw). So #974 collapses from 'design a protocol' to one genuine fork + four FORCED-INVARIANT reconciliations. Forced invariants: (1) conform to/extend the existing servePathIR, do NOT mint a parallel MaaS contract (duplicating the #505 SoT serves drift between two contracts); (2) identity folds the genWrapper PRODUCER VERSION, not CEM-hash-only — protocols.json#maas-versioning makes the version id a content hash over EVERY byte-determining input (definition+transform/compiler version+params+provenance) and names omission 'the one excluded (broken) option'; #974's '[+ CEM hash]' parenthetical is under-specified, correct it; (3) cache = CACHE_POLICY (immutable on hash-pin, 302 on floating); (4) errors = the existing response set + bare-specifier ESM with consumer-side import-map resolution. The FUI endpoint is its OWN runtime conforming to the WE contract (runtime never crosses the WE->FUI seam — #855/#817; FUI cannot import WE fetchHandler runtime), with genWrapper (FUI tooling, #855; pure (decl,target)=>wrapperSource, TARGETS=[react,vue], consume-mode renders the real element) injected as the transform-provider via the existing inject-a-provider seam. THE ONE GENUINE FORK — framework-axis representation: A1 overload the catalog-gated form param (react-wrapper/vue-wrapper as injected FUI catalog values, ZERO neutral-contract change — the IR explicitly declares form values an injected implementation catalog, not the neutral contract) vs A2 add a distinct neutral 'framework' param to servePathIR (framework is orthogonal to form [wrapper vs full-rewrite #818 vs wc-class] and to the ES target; encoding it in form collapses two axes into one enum — the form x framework explosion react-wrapper/vue-wrapper/react-full/vue-full/svelte-wrapper). RECOMMENDED DEFAULT A2 (~65%) on merit (cost is prioritization #465): distinct axes, A2 cleans up the accidentally React-only 'functional' form (form=wrapper, framework=react), matches WE's separate-and-expose-the-whole-axis bias; adds the PARAM (neutral) not the VALUES (still injected). RESIDUAL/red-team: A1 is fully coherent and cheaper, the IR author deliberately kept value-sets out of the neutral contract (over-engineering/YAGNI risk for two frameworks), the 'functional' precedent shows framework-in-form works — HIGH-LEVERAGE, flag for the deciding agent's skeptic pass; the fork's answer also decides whether #974 yields any WE artifact at all (A1->none, A2->one IR param + #506/#507 follow-through). Prior art is ALREADY-RESEARCHED ground (web survey linked not re-run): polyglot-maas-origin #463 (esm.sh-style ESM-over-HTTP serve mechanics), we-fui-wrapper-handoff #855 (@lit/react / Stencil output targets / Lit-Labs gen-wrapper — wrappers are a build-time, CEM-pure artifact, none generate on-demand in the consumer), forward-component-emit-substrate #811 ('Option W mints no new protocol, reuses custom-elements-manifest'), polyglot-live-test-sandbox #955 (the FUI endpoint that is this protocol first caller). Full grounding in reports/2026-06-18-maas-wrapper-serve-protocol.md.

title: Framework best-practices conformance tier — idiomatic React/Vue lint axis for generated wrappers
summary: Prep research for decision #976 (the third graded-conformance tier ratified by #913, under Block Explorer epic #746). #913 ratified a graded model of three honestly-labelled tiers — surface-contract (#975, deterministic), framework best-practices (#976, this lint axis, absent from the original survey), behavioral identity (#967, live mount). This survey grounds tier 2: what 'idiomatic-framework conformance' checks, how WE owns the criteria, and what verdict the badge renders. Grounded in the genWrapper emitters (fui:tools/gen-wrapper/genWrapper.mjs): the React emitter forwards no host ref (keeps its own internal useRef, never exposing it — failing both the pre-19 forwardRef idiom and the React-19 ref-as-prop idiom) and has a depless useEffect; the Vue emitter is mostly idiomatic (defineComponent, typed props, explicit emits array) but misses emit validators. Prior art: React 19 DEPRECATED forwardRef (ref is now a regular prop) — proving idioms shift, so a hardcoded 'must use forwardRef' criterion would be wrong; eslint-plugin-vue (require-prop-types / require-explicit-emits / require-emit-validator) is the Vue idiom authority; eslint-plugin-react / typescript-eslint are ESLint-AST-coupled (idiom checks are static source-pattern matching, unlike #899's engine-neutral behavioral conformance); WCAG A/AA/AAA + ARIA-AT priorities + ESLint error/warn/off establish severity-grading as the pattern for SHOULD-level criteria; custom-elements-everywhere scores behaviorally not by lint (confirming this is a separate axis). Ownership is inherited (criteria->WE, runner->FUI, hosted exerciser->Plateau per #913/#899/constellation-placement) and the honesty rule is inherited (label 'best-practices'/'idiomatic', never 'conformance') — both forced invariants. The criteria SET collapses to a Config-Extends-Platform-Default open registry, and the lint ENGINE choice is FUI impl (mandate-nothing; 'reuse a linter?' is FUI's call, 'cheaper' is prioritization not a fork). The two genuine residual forks: (1) shape of the WE criteria contract — engine-neutral criterion catalog (recommended ~70%, FUI maps each id to a concrete check) vs. an executable ESLint ruleset as the WE standard (bakes a JS-only AST engine into a contract -> lock-in, cuts #855/#817); (2) verdict nature — advisory severity-graded score (recommended ~75%; idioms are SHOULD-level, a binary 'fails idiom = non-conformant' over-claims and ossifies against idiom drift) vs. a binary pass/fail gate symmetric with the two MUST-tiers.

title: MaaS serve-path presets — does a server-named param bundle need a neutral contract surface, or stay an origin-side convenience?
summary: Prep research for decision #979 (Block Explorer epic #746, project webadapters). #979 proposes a server-named PRESET for the MaaS serve path: as the param surface (form/target/strategy plus injected catalog values) grows, a request names one preset id that expands to a fixed param set the origin holds, instead of spelling out every param. Four deferred questions: (1) where it lives (preset query param vs route segment), (2) neutral-contract vs injected catalog like form, (3) composition with explicit params, (4) content-hash identity. HEADLINE FINDING — the same shape #974 just landed: a RECONCILIATION against the existing serve-path IR (blocks/renderers/module-service/servePathIR.ts), recommended path mints NO new WE contract surface. Two of the four questions are FORCED INVARIANTS settled by existing code: (Q4 identity) computeArtifactIdentity (fetchHandler.ts:114-126) hashes the RESOLVED {definitionHash, compilerVersion, params{form,target,strategy}}; a preset is an alias that expands to those params BEFORE identity is computed, so identity is over the EXPANSION not the preset id — protocols.json#maas-versioning names omitting a byte-determining input 'the one excluded (broken) option', and hashing the id string (redefined preset keeps its id while bytes change) is exactly that broken option; (floating alias) an unpinned ?preset=<id> behaves like any floating tag — 302 down the pin ladder (CACHE_POLICY.floating) to the immutable hash of its current expansion, terminal URL carries the expanded params + @pin never the preset id, existing tag->hash machinery reused; (value-set) the preset expansions are an injected origin catalog never enumerated by WE, exactly like form (servePathIR.ts:88-95, neutrality rule :21-24 — WE has no oracle for what react-modern expands to). PRIOR ART — named server-held param bundles are a settled CDN pattern: Cloudflare Images VARIANTS (named option set or comma-list of params; 'a valid URL must specify either a variant or at least one parameter'; flexible variants also accept inline params; path segment), Cloudinary NAMED TRANSFORMATIONS t_<name> (codename for a saved param string c_scale,h_329,w_624->t_preview; slash-chained; composes — chain t_x with inline params, raw_transformation appends literal params; path segment), Akamai IMAGE POLICIES (named server-side bundle by id; query/header policy id; policy + per-request overrides). Lessons: concept validated (vocabulary variant/named-transformation/policy); composition is real and the modern default is OVERRIDE/APPEND (Cloudinary chaining + raw_transformation, Cloudflare flexible variants) with pure-exclusive also in the wild -> Fork B genuine; carrier split in the wild (path vs query) BUT those systems put ALL knobs in the path — WE's grammar deliberately splits identity (<name>[@<pin>]) from selection (query params) and keeps value-sets out of the neutral contract, so a query param is the consistent carrier and a route segment fragments identity, making Fork C's alternative weak FOR OUR GRAMMAR specifically. THE ONE GENUINE FORK — placement against the neutrality seam (mirrors #974 A1/A2): does the bundling mechanism cross the servePathIR neutrality seam, or stay an origin-side convenience? RECOMMENDED DEFAULT (~60%) — a pure origin-side convenience, WE names nothing (parallel to #974's A1), so #979 yields NO WE artifact. SKEPTIC FLAG: the #974 leg-1 conformance-consumption inversion is WEAKER here than in #974 — a preset has one oracle-free, value-set-blind assertion WE COULD own ('?preset=P resolves to the same hash pin as P's spelled-out expansion') that the framework axis lacked, so Fork A is closer to 50/50; flag for the deciding skeptic. Fork B (composition) default ~70% explicit-params-override (most-permissive; Cloudinary/Cloudflare precedent; exclusive + strict-merge are the coherent alternatives). Fork C (carrier, conditional on naming the seam) default ~80% query param not route segment (route is identity-reserved). POSTURE — provisional/deliberately under-committed: the live surface is just three params (servePathIR.ts:138-156), not yet 'unwieldy'; per #974/#978 settle the SHAPE now but gate the neutral-surface build on the #978 cases-log showing real param-list friction; promotion to a WE-named preset param would be a deliberate servePathIR 1.0.0->1.1.0 bump (#979 itself says 'likely a deliberate servePathIR version bump if it lands'). Constellation: neutral contract -> WE (touched only under Fork A's alternative); preset expansion table + parse/expand step + served runtime -> FUI/origin (#855/#817); hosted serve -> plateau-app (#091/#398). Full grounding in reports/2026-06-19-maas-serve-path-presets.md.
